mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL indicates stable economic conditions, reducing uncertainty and supporting equity valuations by ensuring purchasing power stability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently remains below 2.5% YoY but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are predominantly negative (more than 60% of observations) over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic downturns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy. This environment is highly favorable for corporate earnings and thus drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND FEDFUNDS change < 0.1% MoM
regime_hint: economic expansion with stable monetary policy
failure_condition: If periods of strong retail sales growth and stable/declining FEDFUNDS consistently coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, indicating a breakdown of the demand-monetary link.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a high and rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the cost of capital and reduces liquidity in the financial system. This negatively impacts corporate profitability and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently remains above 4.0% during a declared tightening cycle, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly positive (more than 55% of observations) over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong investment and consumer confidence in the economy's future, which typically translates into broader economic activity and increased demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST consistently exceeds 1300k units, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential supply chain disruptions or reduced demand for goods. This negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.3% month-over-month, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues. This robust economic activity translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 100k MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently grows by more than 100k for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly negative (more than 60% of observations) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) coupled with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a robust and efficient economy, where high demand meets full employment. This environment drives strong corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% YoY while UNRATE is below 4.5%, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over any 5-year rolling window.
